channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Por que a candidatura de Caiado pode atrapalhar Flávio no Nordeste
item.description: The potential presidential candidacy of Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) could harm Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) prospects in the Northeast, as allies of ACM Neto (União), the pre-candidate for governor of Bahia, are likely to support Caiado instead. This undermines Flávio's efforts to gain traction in the region, particularly in Bahia—a traditional PT stronghold where he seeks alliances with figures like Neto and Bruno Reis. Although his campaign claims openness to PL supporters, much of Neto's group may align with Caiado, limiting bolsonarist influence.

item.title: Frase do dia
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro criticized the Lula government's stance toward Iran, stating that political support for Tehran places Brazil on the wrong side of a serious conflict and ignores the nature of the regime being defended. He deemed the position unacceptable.

item.title: PoderData: 62% culpam guerra no Irã por alta nos combustíveis
item.description: According to a PoderData poll conducted from March 21 to 23, 2026, 62% of Brazilian voters blame the recent rise in fuel prices on the war in Iran, which began on February 28 with attacks by the U.S. and Israel. Another 26% hold the federal government and President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) responsible, while 8% blame gas stations and 4% did not respond. The survey included 2,500 respondents across 132 municipalities in all 27 federal units, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: Haddad foi “ótimo” ou “bom” para 36% dos eleitores que o conhecem
item.description: According to PoderData survey conducted March 21-23, 36% of voters who know Fernando Haddad rated his performance as Finance Minister as 'excellent' or 'good', while 21% rated it as 'bad' or 'terrible'. Haddad is known by 85% of Brazilians and is a pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo in 2026.

item.title: Decisão do STF libera até R$ 422 mil extrateto por ano a juízes
item.description: The STF ruled that judges and prosecutors may receive up to R$ 421,900 annually in benefits above the constitutional ceiling, allowing up to 70% extra over the current cap of R$ 46,366.19 through penduricalhos and quinquênio. Effective April 2026, the measure excludes health reimbursements, 13th salary, vacation bonuses, and similar payments. The decision was based on a joint vote by ministers Zanin, Dino, Moraes, and read by dean Gilmar Mendes.

item.title: Governo manda Zap para 8 milhões retirarem botijão do Gás do Povo
item.description: The federal government is sending WhatsApp messages to 8 million new beneficiaries of the Gás do Povo program, instructing them on how to access the benefit via the Meu Social-Gás do Povo app or the Disque Social 121 hotline. Launched in September 2025, the program replaces the Auxílio Gás dos Brasileiros and now covers 14.8 million families (44.6 million people), with plans to reach 50 million. Funded with R$ 5.1 billion from the 2026 budget, Gás do Povo is a central pillar of President Lula’s re-election campaign in 2026, particularly due to its strong presence in Brazil’s largest electoral colleges.

item.title: Mercosul faz 35 anos sob tensão política e expectativa com acordo europeu
item.description: Mercosul turns 35 marked by incomplete integration, limited to a partial customs union with weak macroeconomic coordination and strong dependence on the political orientations of member states' leaders. Experts argue that the bloc's failure to advance toward a true common market stems from reluctance to cede sovereignty in monetary, fiscal, exchange rate, and capital/person flows, compounded by the absence of a supranational body to prioritize bloc interests over national ones, leaving it vulnerable to government shifts and global fragmentation.

item.title: O Rio de Janeiro continua lindo visto de cima. Visto de baixo, fede
item.description: Rio de Janeiro faces a cycle of governors convicted or involved in corruption scandals, from Sérgio Cabral to Cláudio Castro, who resigned to avoid TSE conviction for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections and became ineligible for eight years. Despite convictions, many former governors, including Cabral, Pezão, and Rosinha Garotinho, remain free and active in public life, some returning to elected office or becoming political commentators. The article criticizes the apathetic voting behavior of Rio’s electorate over 30 years, which perpetuates a pattern of leadership tied to illegality.

item.title: Moraes vai decidir sobre afastamento de governador desafeto de Dino no Maranhão
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes is expected to rule soon on whether to remove Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão (PSB), who is accused by allies of Flávio Dino of violating court orders related to nepotism cases. Brandão’s defense denies the allegations, calling the effort politically motivated, while Dino’s faction claims Brandão continues to employ relatives — including his brother Marcos Brandão, Ítalo Reis, and Gilberto Lins — using state funds. The case unfolds amid intense political and legal battles ahead of the 2026 elections, with Lieutenant Governor Felipe Camarão (PT) also facing a removal request over alleged money laundering, which he denies as political persecution.

item.title: Dario Durigan: o que esperar do ministro da Fazenda, em meio à guerra, diesel caro e ano eleitoral
item.description: President Lula has confirmed Dario Durigan as the new Minister of Finance, replacing Fernando Haddad, who is leaving to run for governor of São Paulo. Durigan, formerly the deputy minister, assumes the role with a focus on maintaining fiscal continuity, particularly adhering to the fiscal arc and controlling inflation amid Middle East-driven diesel price increases and an election year. His management is expected to prioritize predictability, avoiding abrupt changes and reinforcing commitment to already-defined fiscal targets, without pursuing major innovations.

item.title: TSE determina eleição indireta para novo governador do RJ
item.description: The TSE ruled that Rio de Janeiro will hold an indirect election for governor and vice-governor, with the state legislature (Alerj) responsible for the vote, following the ineligibility of Claudio Castro until 2030 and the resignation of both executive officials. The president of the Rio de Janeiro State Court of Justice, Ricardo Couto de Castro, is currently serving as interim governor until the indirect election takes place.

item.title: Lei Antifacção: apenas 1,4% dos presos recebem auxílio-reclusão
item.description: The Anti-Crime Law, signed by Lula, restricts the prison allowance for inmates linked to violent criminal organizations, but its impact is limited: only 1.4% of the 496,506 prisoners in closed or semi-open regime receive the benefit, totaling R$11.7 million in 2025. The allowance is paid to low-income dependents of inmates who contributed to Social Security for at least 24 months and meet specific requirements.

item.title: Defesa de Lulinha e Centrão apostam que STF vetará prorrogação da CPMI
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha) and Centrão leaders believe the STF plenary will block the extension of the INSS CPMI, which is being judged this Thursday (March 26). The commission, originally set to end this week, investigates the president’s son and businessmen linked to bloc parliamentarians. The STF decision will benefit Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who opposes the extension and awaits the ruling before complying with Minister André Mendonça’s monocratic decision.

item.title: Inelegível pelo TSE, Castro ainda pode disputar o Senado? Entenda os recursos possíveis
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL-RJ) was declared ineligible for eight years by the TSE for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, but he may still run for Senate in 2026 if he obtains a suspensive effect through legal appeals, such as embargos of declaration to the TSE and an extraordinary appeal to the STF, although ineligibility is not automatically lifted by filing appeals alone. Experts state that without a favorable decision suspending the penalty, the Electoral Justice is likely to reject his candidacy registration.

item.title: Lula cobra que Tarcísio reconheça dinheiro federal em obras de SP
item.description: Lula urged Governor Tarcísio de Freitas to acknowledge federal funding in São Paulo infrastructure projects, stating that federal financial support is an obligation, not a favor. He cited the Santos-Guarujá submerged tunnel as an example where half the funding comes from the federal government, and criticized the nation being built on lies. The remarks were made during the inauguration of a CRRC factory in Araraquara, amid Fernando Haddad’s official pre-candidacy for São Paulo governor and the state election’s importance to Lula’s 2026 re-election bid.

item.title: Guilherme Afif lança obra sobre sua trajetória política e empresarial
item.description: Guilherme Afif launched his memoir 'Juntos chegaremos lá!' in São Paulo, highlighting his political and business trajectory, including his role in the 1987-1988 Constituent Assembly advocating for microenterprises in the Constitution, leadership at the São Paulo Commercial Association and Sebrae, and support for Tarcísio de Freitas's re-election. The event featured Eduardo Leite and Ronaldo Caiado, whom Afif described as 'two excellent candidacies' for the PSD in 2026.

item.title: Latam incorpora na frota os primeiros aviões da Embraer
item.description: Latam received the first 24 E195-E2 aircraft from Embraer, marking the first time Latin America’s largest airline uses planes manufactured by Brazil’s aerospace industry. The US$2.1 billion deal is part of a broader US$4 billion investment plan from 2023 to 2026 to expand fleet and domestic and international connectivity, with an option to acquire up to 50 additional jets.

item.title: Como funcionam viaturas com reconhecimento facial anunciadas por Tarcísio
item.description: The São Paulo state government announced the acquisition of 1,173 new police vehicles equipped with facial recognition and license plate reading cameras, integrated into the Muralha Paulista and Smart Sampa systems, with an investment of R$173 million. The first batch of 258 vehicles has already been delivered to the Civil and Military Police, operating in the capital, Greater São Paulo, and inland cities. The AI-powered system generates real-time alerts when it identifies wanted individuals or stolen vehicles.

item.title: Farra na Segurança: Assembleia aprova projeto que cria delegados sem delegacia e promoção automática
item.description: São Paulo’s Legislative Assembly approved a bill by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas establishing automatic promotion of police officers without merit or vacancy requirements, while limiting tenure in leadership roles for senior delegates to 12 years. The measure was rejected by the Civil Police leadership, who argue it violates reasonableness, breaks parity with the Military Police, risks creating officers without functions, and may serve political patronage networks.

item.title: Leite mira em Caiado e pressiona Kassab por candidatura de centro
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD-RS) urged the PSD to field a centrist presidential candidate in 2026, opposing alignment with the Bolsonarist camp and directly naming Ronaldo Caiado (PSD-GO) as an internal rival for the party’s nomination. Leite also pressured PSD national president Gilberto Kassab to clarify the party’s stance, warning against drifting too far toward the far right.

item.title: Pesquisa traz Flávio Bolsonaro superando Lula no segundo turno
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro leads Lula by one percentage point in a projected presidential runoff, with 47.6% versus 46.6% in the AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll. The senator, son of former president Jair Bolsonaro, had previously been tied with Lula at 46.3% to 46.2%, signaling a gradual upward trend in his electoral support.

item.title: Guilherme Afif defende reeleição de Tarcísio em SP
item.description: Guilherme Afif defended the re-election of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas in São Paulo, emphasizing the importance of administrative continuity to maintain the government's strong performance. He also criticized national economic policy, warning of fiscal crisis risks between 2026 and 2027 due to rising public spending and debt, and advocated for tax simplification to support micro and small businesses.

item.title: Lei da misoginia: opinião pode virar crime?
item.description: A proposed anti-misogyny law in Brazil has sparked debate over whether opinions opposing feminist movements could be criminalized. Duda Teixeira, Madeleine Lacsko, and Carlos Graieb discuss whether such legislation would threaten free speech or be a necessary measure against gender-based hatred. The controversy arises amid a polarized political climate, with recent polls showing Flávio Bolsonaro ahead of Lula in some second-round scenarios, according to AtlasIntel.

item.title: Eduardo diz que Moraes teme morte de Bolsonaro
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro claimed that Minister Alexandre de Moraes granted house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro out of fear for his death, not legal grounds, calling the decision political and aimed at protecting the STF's image, which he described as worn and scandal-ridden. He argued his father should not have been prosecuted for the January 8 events, noting he was in the U.S. and no longer president. Moraes’ 90-day measure is based on Bolsonaro’s health condition and a favorable opinion from the PGR.

item.title: Endividamento das famílias virou risco eleitoral
item.description: In the final year of his third term, President Lula launched a campaign against record household indebtedness in Brazil, after observing that key economic achievements — declining inflation, economic growth, and record-low unemployment — are not being felt by the population.

item.title: Estudantes ocupam prédio da Secretaria da Educação de SP em protesto contra a gestão Tarcísio
item.description: Students have occupied a room at the São Paulo State Secretariat of Education in protest against Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' education policies, including the implementation of civic-military schools, closure of night education, and platformization of teaching. The demonstration, led by the Union of Secondary Students of São Paulo (Upes), involves over 20 students from various cities demanding a school reorganization that respects the educational community. Despite negotiation attempts by Seduc and military police, protesters remain in the building as of 7:30 p.m. on Wednesday, March 25, 2026.

item.title: STF aprova tese que unifica teto salarial e elimina penduricalhos para magistratura e MP
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) approved a thesis that unifies the salary cap and eliminates 'penduricalhos' (extra benefits) for judges and members of the Public Prosecutor's Office, reaffirming the constitutional ceiling of R$ 46.366,19. The ruling declares unconstitutional benefits created by administrative resolutions, state laws, or local judicial decisions, including natal, fuel, housing, and food allowances, as well as compensatory leaves and preschool assistance. The new rules take effect starting with the March base month, impacting April payroll, and mandate full transparency in compensation.

item.title: TSE define que escolha de novo governador do Rio será por eleição indireta
item.description: The TSE has determined that the new governor of Rio de Janeiro will be chosen through an indirect election, scheduled for April 25, 2026. The decision follows the resignation and conviction of Cláudio Castro and the ineligibility of Rodrigo Bacellar, leaving the state without a vice-governor or a constitutionally valid successor. The TRE of Rio will be responsible for urgently communicating the decision and for the retotalization of votes for state deputy.

item.title: Leite alerta Kassab: ‘Não pode ser uma candidatura na esquerda e três na direita’
item.description: Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD-RS) stated that the PSD's 2026 presidential candidacy must be a centrist option, avoiding debates on amnesty and positioning itself as a real alternative to political polarization, indirectly criticizing the right-leaning profile of Ronaldo Caiado (PSD-GO), who supports amnesty for those involved in the January 8 attacks. Leite emphasized his public security achievements in Rio Grande do Sul as credentials to combat organized crime and argued that Brazil should not have to choose between toughness on crime and protection for the vulnerable. He also stressed the need to restore institutional moral authority, citing the Banco Master scandal as another example of recurring political crises that undermine electoral credibility.

item.title: Após reunião com Leite, Kassab diz que candidato será anunciado até terça e que não há favorito
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab said the party will announce its presidential pre-candidate by next Tuesday (21), with governors Ronaldo Caiado (GO) and Eduardo Leite (RS) as the main contenders. He denied there is a frontrunner, stating the decision is not yet made and will be based on political, not technical, criteria. Leite said he left the meeting with Kassab 'encouraged' and believes his profile allows broader dialogue with the Brazilian electorate in a polarized context.

item.title: Kassab se demitiu do governo de SP por mensagem; encontro com Tarcísio será quinta
item.description: Gilberto Kassab resigned as São Paulo's Institutional Relations Secretary via message, without meeting Governor Tarcísio de Freitas. A meeting is scheduled for Thursday to ease tensions and discuss maintaining Felicio Ramuth as the vice-presidential candidate. Kassab had sought the vice spot, but Tarcísio has already affirmed his support for Felicio Ramuth.

item.title: Lula erra ao dizer que Brasil faz fronteira com toda a América do Sul
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva made an error by claiming Brazil borders all countries in South America, overlooking that Chile and Ecuador do not share a land border with Brazil. The statement was made during a visit to Latam's maintenance center in São Carlos, where Lula also praised the airline's purchase of Embraer aircraft. The event featured government officials and Latam executives.

item.title: Alckmin diz que pesquisa é fotografia do momento: “No momento certo, população fará julgamento”
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin downplayed polls showing Flávio Bolsonaro ahead of Lula, stating electoral surveys are merely a 'snapshot of the moment' and expressing confidence that Brazilians will make their judgment at the right time, citing advances in health, economy, deforestation reduction, and social programs. He announced he will step down from the MDIC ministry next week to comply with electoral law, while remaining Vice President. The Atlas/Bloomberg poll showed Bolsonaro at 47.6% and Lula at 46.6%, technically tied within the margin of error.

item.title: Eleição para mandato-tampão ao Governo do Rio será indireta, diz TSE
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) confirmed that the election for the interim governorship of Rio de Janeiro will be indirect, conducted by the state Legislative Assembly (Alerj), following the resignation of former Governor Cláudio Castro (PL). The decision came after Castro anticipated his declaration of ineligibility by the Court. The interim term will be filled until the 2026 general elections.

item.title: TSE confirma que eleição-tampão no Rio será indireta
item.description: The TSE confirmed that the election to complete Cláudio Castro's gubernatorial term in Rio de Janeiro will be indirect, decided by the State Legislative Assembly, not by popular vote. The ruling affirms the legal interpretation that in cases of gubernatorial vacancy, succession must occur via indirect election among state deputies.

item.title: O eixo Lula-Bolsonaro até aqui resiste a uma alternativa
item.description: The Lula-Bolsonaro axis remains the only viable electoral duel in 2026 polls, despite widespread desire for an alternative outside this polarization. The lack of a center candidate with significant support reflects the weakness of Brazilian party institutions, described as a 'democracy without parties' focused on looting the treasury. While dissatisfaction with the status quo fuels hopes for a 'spontaneous combustion' via social media, no name has yet emerged to break the polarization.

item.title: Governador em exercício consulta TSE sobre eleição direta ou indireta no RJ
item.description: Acting Governor of Rio de Janeiro, Justice Ricardo Couto, submitted an inquiry to the TSE seeking clarification on whether the upcoming gubernatorial election following Claudio Castro's resignation should be direct or indirect. The request aims to resolve the succession process in the state, which hinges on interpreting electoral law regarding the vacancy.

item.title: Lindbergh aciona STF contra Moro por fala sobre eleição de 2022
item.description: Federal Deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) filed a factual complaint with the STF against Senator Sérgio Moro (PL-PR), alleging that Moro’s statements about the 2022 election may constitute criminal offenses such as abuse of economic power or responsibility crime. The request was sent to Minister Alexandre de Moraes, rapporteur of the digital militias inquiry, to investigate whether Moro’s remarks amount to electoral violations. The goal is to uphold electoral integrity and hold accountable those seeking to undermine the legitimacy of the vote.

item.title: TSE confirma eleição indireta para governador do RJ
item.description: The TSE confirmed that the succession of the Rio de Janeiro governorship following Cláudio Castro’s resignation will be decided by an indirect election in the state Legislative Assembly (Alerj), correcting a material error in a prior ruling that had raised doubts about a direct vote. The decision aligns with Article 142, §1º of the Rio State Constitution, which mandates indirect elections in case of vacancy during the final years of the term. Acting Governor Ricardo Couto has 48 hours to call the election, which must be held within 30 days, with the 70 state deputies voting in an extraordinary session, requiring an absolute majority of 36 votes to win in the first round.

item.title: Lula agradece Latam por compra de 24 aviões da Embraer
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva thanked Latam for purchasing 24 Embraer aircraft, announced at an event in São Carlos (SP), emphasizing the importance of Brazilian-made planes to boost tourism and international trade, particularly with Africa. Lula stressed that Brazil must actively pursue business abroad rather than wait for foreigners to come. Minister of Ports and Airports Silvio Costa Filho stated Latam may buy up to 64 aircraft, with the current deal exceeding R$ 10 billion, and that the government aims to reach 140 million air passengers.

item.title: Lula sonda integrantes da ala lulista sobre a filiação de Pacheco ao MDB para candidatura em Minas
item.description: Lula probed lulist allies within the MDB about the potential affiliation of Senator Rodrigo Pacheco to the party for a Minas Gerais gubernatorial run. He sought an overview of the MDB’s state-level situation and heard that party members are open to the idea but stressed that any decision must honor existing agreements, as Gabriel Azevedo is the current pre-candidate. Lula also discussed electoral scenarios in Bahia and Alagoas, without mentioning a vice-presidential offer to the MDB.

item.title: Lulinha consultor da Fictor?
item.description: Fábio Luís da Silva, known as Lulinha, son of President Lula (PT), worked as a consultant for the Fictor group in 2024, according to Folha de S.Paulo. His work was discreet, with limited visits to the group’s offices, as reported by two sources. The hiring aimed to bring the group closer to political influence.

item.title: STJ retomará julgamento de governador do Acre após renúncia
item.description: The STJ will resume on April 15 the trial of Acre Governor Gladson Cameli, who resigned on March 24 to run for a Senate seat. He faces charges of organized crime, active and passive corruption, embezzlement, money laundering, and bid-rigging. The Special Court will decide whether the case stays in the STJ or is transferred to the STF if he is elected senator.

item.title: Lindbergh aciona o STF após Moro insinuar que vitória de Lula em 2022 foi ilegítima
item.description: Federal Deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) filed a formal complaint with the STF against Senator Sergio Moro (PL-PR) for suggesting that Lula's 2022 election victory was illegitimate. Lindbergh argues that Moro's statement undermines public trust in institutions and perpetuates the symbolic narrative of electoral fraud. Minister Alexandre de Moraes is the rapporteur of the case, which falls under the Digital Militias Inquiry.

item.title: Lula sugere que Camilo Santana pode ser candidato: ‘se precisar’
item.description: President Lula suggested that Education Minister Camilo Santana could run for governor of Ceará in 2026 if needed, despite not currently being a pre-candidate. The statement was made during the inauguration of new areas at the UFSCar University Hospital, amid PT concerns over Ciro Gomes’ (PSDB) lead in polls over Elmano de Freitas (PT). Lula also emphasized that investments in health, education, and universities should be treated as strategic state investments, not mere expenditures.

item.title: Pressão da indústria trava discussão de projeto sobre fim da escala 6×1 na Câmara
item.description: The postponement of the bill to end the 6×1 work scale in the Chamber, led by Deputy Zé Adriano (PP-AC), reflects industry pressure to avoid voting in an election year. He argues that reducing the workweek from 44 to 40 hours requires careful analysis of productivity and competitiveness impacts, especially for micro and small businesses, and advocates for negotiation and government compensation, criticizing the Labor Minister's stance as 'too radical'.

item.title: Senado aprova projeto que cria 794 novos cargos e funções na Justiça Eleitoral
item.description: The Senate approved a bill creating 794 new positions and functions in the Electoral Justice, including 232 judicial analysts, 242 judicial technicians, 75 commissioned positions, and 245 commissioned functions. Proposed by the TSE and approved without changes, the measure aims to address voter growth, increased candidacies, judicial and extrajudicial processes, election security demands, disinformation combat, and CNJ compliance. Funding depends on the Annual Budget Law (LOA), Budget Guidelines Law (LDO), and a projected R$105 million allocation for 2026.

item.title: O que Flávio ainda precisa mostrar para consolidar liderança contra Lula, segundo analista da AtlasIntel
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro technically leads Lula with 47.6% to 46.6% in voting intentions, according to AtlasIntel, but the lead is within the margin of error and relies on vote transfer from Jair Bolsonaro. To consolidate his lead, he must demonstrate independent governing capacity beyond his bolsonarist base, appealing to politically disengaged voters and proving leadership attributes, as his lack of executive experience remains a key vulnerability.

item.title: Ibovespa sobe mais de 1% com possível cessar-fogo no Oriente Médio
item.description: The Ibovespa rose 1.60% to 185,400 points, driven by the prospect of a possible ceasefire between the U.S., Israel, and Iran, which reduced global fear and increased risk appetite. Domestically, the AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll showed Lula’s disapproval rising to 53.5%, favoring pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro and pleasing the market. Banking stocks led gains, with Bradesco (BBDC4) up 1.80% and Itaú (ITUB4) up 1.32%.

item.title: Após inelegibilidade e convite do PP, Valdemar diz que Castro permanece candidato do PL ao Senado
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) reaffirmed former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro as its Senate candidate, despite his ineligibility declared by the TSE until 2030. PL national president Valdemar Costa Neto declared unrestricted support and stated the party will work to reverse the court's decision. The statement came hours after the Progressistas (PP) invited Castro to join the party, following his conviction for irregular use of temporary contracts at UERJ and Ceperj to pay campaign staff.
